title: “If this country is ever to rise from the ashes even a generation or two later, majoritarian sentiment fed by hate-mongering against Tamil and Muslim minorities must be recognised and rejected for what it is. That is where national healing must start.”
body_lines: From Mullaitivu to Kurunegala to Athurigiriya, fury erupts among snaking lines of Sri Lankans hopelessly waiting for days on end for fuel. Finally, unity binds the Sinhalese, the Tamils and the Muslims in their collectively deep anger against a Government that has forsaken public trust. But is this too late as the country heads into dangerously uncharted waters of non-governance with dollar and rupee reserves reduced to zero due to a cabal of thieving and incompetent political leaders along with their obedient henchmen in public service?

Across the length and breadth of the land, there is a discernible uptick in tensions as the police react with uncontrolled force against public protests. Is the Government inviting another deadly wave of public anger? This week, a (Tamil) journalist was attacked by an unidentified person while filming scenes of people waiting in lines for petrol on the Mankulam-Vellankulam road with the police looking on.

In Kurunegala, a (Sinhala) motorcyclist was literally sought to be throttled by a policeman even as the victim shouted that the particular fuel station concerned is distributing fuel in secret only to the ‘privileged.’

Interestingly a senior policeman reprimanded people flocking to the fracas to the effect that, ‘do not take the incidents of May 9th as an example to cause violence.’ This statement exposes the way that the State is authorising its agents to ‘manage’ and ‘control’ the acute crisis of law, economy and governance that is throttling Sri Lanka.

In effect, it is not just one stray citizen who is sought to be throttled by one stray policeman overstepping the bounds of his legal authority.Rather, this is a powerful symbol of the suffocation of the country itself as President Gotabhaya Rajapaksa, Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe and Ministerial minions downwards practice the art of deftly deflecting blame to Sri Lankan citizens for their misery.
On his part, Prime Minister Wickremesinghe cannot escape responsibility merely by parroting that the present financial collapse was due to ruinous policy decisions during the past two years.

He must be summarily reminded that the return of the Rajapaksas to power as Sri Lanka’s ‘King-Politicians’ with grossly bloated majorities in 2019 and 2020 was due to disastrous governance failures under the Wickremesinghe-Sirisena coalition leadership (2015-2019). The 2019 jihadist attacks on churches and hotels with the inaction if not direct complicity of state agents led to the Rajapaksas amassing their electoral majorities with gleeful force.

That responsibility is far more stark than what can be attributed to any former leader, though each have contributed his or her fair share towards Sri Lanka’s present plight. Regardless, Sri Lankans (innocent and guilty alike) are now paying dearly for their gullibility in being fooled not once, twice, thrice but many times by crafty political leaders.

This was conceded as much by former Finance Minister Basil Rajapaksa on his way out from his ministerial portfolio when he said that the people who elected the current crop of politicians to power are to blame.

For the people of the South who worshipped the Medamulana political family led by its paterfamilias, Mahinda Rajapaksa because he ‘ended the war,’ the cost is particularly high. As families starve in the deep South, hungry looking children stand on roadsides with their mothers selling straggly bunches of drumsticks to survive. Has even a trace of self-realisation dawned that the originators of this crisis are none other than themselves? I am not quite sure, even now.

But if this country is ever to rise from the ashes even a generation or two later, majoritarian sentiment fed by hate-mongering against Tamil and Muslim minorities must be recognised and rejected for what it is. That is where national healing must start.

A few days ago, Sri Lankan media reported that Dr. Shafi Shihabdeen had donated his salary arrears of Sri Lanka Rs 2.67 million back to the Kurunegala Teaching Hospital which had falsely accused him of performing illegal sterilization operations. This donation was to buy essential medicines.

At the time, he was witchhunted by a coterie of racist politicians, hospital personnel and conniving policemen and sent on compulsory leave. These allegations were established to be completely unfounded by the Criminal Investigations Department (CID).

To add insult to injury, some women who had accused him of illegally sterilizing them, later became pregnant through some miracle, we assume. Members of the clergy (Buddhist and Catholic in some instances) also jumped on the racist bandwagon, privately if not publicly.

This was the ugliness that Sri Lanka had become, long before we were precipitated into this mess of a bankrupt, beggarly nation. One may indeed argue that this is cause and effect in the best sense of what that means.

The Shafi Shihabdeen incident is symptomatic of what is wrong in ourselves. Reforming Constitutions will not help to correct that imbalance. Waving the racist flag, a little known ‘Viyathmaga’ academic of the Rajarata University rose to national political prominence, later becoming an object of ridicule in his ministerial duties.

Partners in that unholy conspiracy, responsible hospital personnel and policemen went their way unscathed by the law while the victimised medical professional and his family found sheer existence impossible in the face of the venom directed against them. What role was played by professional associations bound to speak up to safeguard the integrity of their members? Did the Sri Lanka College Of Obstetricians & Gynecologists (SLCOG)make any significant intervention in this regard? Less said about the disgracefully politicised Government Medical Officers Association  (GMOA), the better.

Hopefully the recent change at the helm of the GMOA will bring about professional integrity in its leadership. As this nation collapses in utter grief and pain within itself today, these are contributory reasons for our plight.
Why has not an inch of accountability been conceded by political rulers distinguished only by their monumental greed and thirst for power?

The arrests of a few Ministers and their supporters who instigated violent attacks on peaceful protestors at Galle Face Green and the departure of former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa are but sops thrown to placate howls for justice.

Meanwhile, protestors are arrested, threatened and intimidated as part of an overall State operation to clamp down on dissent. On their part, youth protestors should also have issued unequivocal condemnation of the death of one parliamentarian, the merciless assault of another and the destruction of public property during the violence of May 9th, some of which was however by state agents themselves or as a result of internal political vendettas.

Nonetheless, the shifting of the moral high ground from under the feet of protestors emanating from the events of that day was well reflected in the warning issued by the Kurunegala policemen to the angry crowd. This is the modus operandi of the State, going forward.
And the President continues undeterred in office on the basis that he cannot leave as a ‘failed’ leader. This is an absurd justification, devoid of both commonsense and constitutional principle.

Presidential course reversal is certainly not evidenced in the catapulting of a businessman with his fat fingers in all kinds of financial pies, through the National List to Parliament in an indecent hurry as it were. This does not demonstrate the slightest adherence to constitutional democracy.

On the contrary, it flies in the face of precedents clearly laid down by the Supreme Court to the effect that National List slots cannot be filled at the whim and fancy of the political establishment. All of these appointments are akin to magic tricks performed by circus acrobats to please an unimpressed audience.
Is this how the President hopes to ‘redeem’ himself?  Truly, we shudder in dire expectation of what the future holds.

si.title: “පරම්පරාවකට හෝ දෙකකට පසුව හෝ මේ රට අළුතින් නැඟී සිටීමට නම්, දෙමළ සහ මුස්ලිම් සුළු ජාතීන්ට එරෙහිව වෛරය අවුස්සමින් පෝෂණය කරන බහුතර හැඟීම් හඳුනාගෙන එය ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ යුතුය. ජාතික සුවය ආරම්භ කළ යුත්තේ එතැනින්.”
body_lines: මුලතිව් සිට කුරුණෑගල සිට අතුරුගිරිය දක්වා ඉන්ධන සඳහා දින ගණන් බලාපොරොත්තු විරහිතව බලා සිටින ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන්ගේ සර්ප රේඛා අතර කෝපය පුපුරා යයි. අවසාන වශයෙන්, එකමුතුකම නිසා සිංහල, දෙමළ සහ මුස්ලිම් ජනතාව මහජන විශ්වාසය අත්හැර දැමූ රජයකට එරෙහිව සාමූහික ගැඹුරු කෝපයක් ඇති කරයි. එහෙත් රාජ්‍ය සේවයේ යෙදී සිටින ඔවුන්ගේ කීකරු හෙන්චයියන් සමඟ සොරුන් සහ අදක්ෂ දේශපාලන නායකයින් පිරිසක් නිසා ඩොලර් සහ රුපියල සංචිත බිංදුව දක්වා පහත වැටුණු රට පාලනය නොවන භයානක දියවර කරා ගමන් කරන විට මෙය ප්‍රමාද වැඩිද?

මහජන විරෝධතාවලට එරෙහිව පොලිසිය පාලනය කළ නොහැකි බලයකින් ප්‍රතික්‍රියා කිරීම නිසා භූමියේ දිග පළල, ආතතීන්හි කැපී පෙනෙන වර්ධනයක් පවතී. මහජන කෝපයේ තවත් මාරාන්තික රැල්ලකට රජය ආරාධනා කරනවාද? මේ සතියේ මාන්කුලම් - වෙල්ලන්කුලම් පාරේ පෙට්‍රල් සඳහා පෝලිම්වල රැඳී සිටින මිනිසුන් පොලිසිය බලා සිටින ආකාරය රූගත කරමින් සිටියදී (දෙමළ) මාධ්‍යවේදියකුට නාඳුනන පුද්ගලයකු විසින් පහර දෙන ලදී.
කුරුණෑගලදී (සිංහල) යතුරුපැදිකරුවෙකු පොලිස් නිලධාරියෙකු විසින් වචනානුසාරයෙන් තෙරපීමට උත්සාහ කළේ අදාළ ඉන්ධන පිරවුම්හල රහසිගතව ‘වරප්‍රසාදලාභීන්ට’ පමණක් ඉන්ධන බෙදා හරින බවට වින්දිතයා කෑගසද්දී ය.

ප්‍රචණ්ඩත්වය ඇති කිරීමට මැයි 9 වැනි දින සිදුවීම් උදාහරණයක් ලෙස නොසලකන ලෙසට ජ්‍යෙෂ්ඨ පොලිස් නිලධාරියෙක් තරවටු කරන ලද ජනතාවට තරවටු කිරීම සිත්ගන්නා කරුණකි. මෙම ප්‍රකාශය මගින් රාජ්‍යය තම නියෝජිතයින්ට 'කළමනාකරණය' කිරීමට සහ '' කිරීමට බලය පවරන ආකාරය හෙළිදරව් කරයි. ශ්‍රී ලංකාව යටපත් කරමින් පවතින නීතියේ, ආර්ථිකයේ සහ පාලනයේ උග්‍ර අර්බුදය පාලනය කිරීම.

ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම, එක් අයාලේ යන පොලිස් නිලධාරියෙකු විසින් තම නීතිමය අධිකාරියේ සීමාවන් ඉක්මවා යන අතරමං කිරීමට උත්සාහ කරන්නේ එක් අයාලේ යන පුරවැසියෙකු පමණක් නොවේ. ඒ වෙනුවට, මෙය ජනාධිපති ගෝඨාභය රාජපක්ෂ, අගමැති රනිල් ලෙස රටම හුස්ම හිරවීමේ ප්‍රබල සංකේතයකි. වික්‍රමසිංහ සහ ඇමති ගෝල බාලයන් ශ්‍රී ලාංකේය පුරවැසියන්ගේ දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයන් පිළිබඳව දොස් පැවරීම ඉතා දක්ෂ ලෙස ප්‍රගුණ කරති.
වත්මන් මූල්‍ය කඩාවැටීමට පසුගිය දෙවසර තුළ ගත් විනාශකාරී ප්‍රතිපත්තිමය තීරණ නිසා යැයි ගිරවුන් කී පමණින් අගමැති වික්‍රමසිංහට වගකීමෙන් මිදිය නොහැක.

2019 සහ 2020 දී දැඩි ලෙස පුම්බාගත් බහුතරයක් සහිතව රාජපක්ෂවරුන් ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ ‘රජ-දේශපාලකයන්’ ලෙස නැවත බලයට පැමිණීම වික්‍රමසිංහ-සිරිසේන සභාග නායකත්වය (2015-2019) යටතේ සිදු වූ ව්‍යසනකාරී පාලන අසාර්ථකත්වය නිසා බව ඔහුට සාරාංශයෙන් මතක් කළ යුතුය. 2019 පල්ලි සහ හෝටල්වලට එල්ල කළ ජිහාඩ් ප්‍රහාර, රාජ්‍ය ඒජන්තවරුන්ගේ අකර්මණ්‍ය භාවයත් සමඟ සෘජුවම නොඑසේ නම්, රාජපක්ෂලා තම මැතිවරණ බහුතරයක් ප්‍රීතිමත් බලයෙන් රැස් කර ගැනීමට හේතු විය.

ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ වත්මන් දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයට සෑම කෙනෙකුම තමාගේ හෝ ඇයගේ සාධාරණ කොටස දායක කර ඇතත්, ඕනෑම හිටපු නායකයෙකුට ආරෝපණය කළ හැකි වගකීමට වඩා එම වගකීම ඉතා පැහැදිලිය. කෙසේ වෙතත්, ශ්‍රී ලාංකිකයන් (අහිංසක හා වැරදිකරුවන්) දැන් කපටි දේශපාලන නායකයින් විසින් වරක්, දෙවරක්, තුන් වතාවක්, බොහෝ වාරයක් නොමඟ යැවීමේ ඔවුන්ගේ ගුප්ත භාවය වෙනුවෙන් බොහෝ සේ ගෙවමින් සිටිති.
හිටපු මුදල් අමාත්‍ය බැසිල් රාජපක්ෂ මහතා සිය අමාත්‍ය ධූරයෙන් ඉවත්ව යන අතරමගදී මේ බව පිළිගත්තේ වත්මන් දේශපාලකයින් බලයට පත් කළ ජනතාව බලයට පත් කළ ජනතාව බව පවසමිනි.

මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ ‘යුද්ධය අවසන් කළ’ නිසා මැදමූලන දේශපාලන පවුල ප්‍රමුඛ මැදමූලන දේශපාලන පවුලට වැඳුම් පිදුම් කළ දකුණේ ජනතාවට ඒ සඳහා වැය වන මුදල විශේෂයෙන් වැඩිය. ගැඹුරු දකුණේ පවුල් සාගින්නෙන් පෙළෙන විට, කුසගින්නෙන් පෙළෙන දරුවන් තම මව්වරුන් සමඟ පාර අයිනේ සිටගෙන දිවි ගලවා ගැනීම සඳහා මුරුංගා පොකුරු විකුණති. මේ අර්බුදයේ මූලාරම්භකයන් අන් කිසිවකු නොව තමන් බව ස්වයං අවබෝධය පිළිබඳ අංශු මාත්‍රයක් හෝ පහළ වී තිබේද? මට හරියටම විශ්වාස නැහැ, දැන් පවා.

නමුත් පරම්පරාවකට හෝ දෙකකට පසුව හෝ මේ රට අළුතින් නැඟී සිටීමට නම්, දෙමළ සහ මුස්ලිම් සුළු ජාතීන්ට එරෙහිව වෛරී වෛරයෙන් පෝෂණය වන බහුතර හැඟීම් එය කුමක්දැයි හඳුනාගෙන ප්‍රතික්ෂේප කළ යුතුය. ජාතික සුවය ආරම්භ කළ යුත්තේ එතැනිනි.

දින කිහිපයකට පෙර වෛද්‍ය ෂාෆි ෂිහාබ්දීන් නීතිවිරෝධී වඳ සැත්කම් කළ බවට අසත්‍ය චෝදනා කළ කුරුණෑගල ශික්ෂණ රෝහලට ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ හිඟ වැටුප් රුපියල් මිලියන 2.67 පරිත්‍යාග කර ඇති බව ශ්‍රී ලංකාවේ මාධ්‍ය වාර්තා කළේය. මෙම පරිත්‍යාගය අත්‍යාවශ්‍ය ඖෂධ මිලදී ගැනීමටය.

එකල ඔහු ජාතිවාදී දේශපාලකයින්, රෝහල් සේවකයින් සහ පොලිසියට අනුබල දුන් කණ්ඩායමක් විසින් දඩයම් කර අනිවාර්ය නිවාඩු යවන ලදී. මෙම චෝදනා සම්පූර්ණයෙන්ම පදනම් විරහිත බව අපරාධ පරීක්ෂණ දෙපාර්තමේන්තුව (CID) විසින් තහවුරු කරන ලදී.

තවත් අපහාසයක් ලෙස, ඔහුට නීති විරෝධී ලෙස වඳ කළ බවට චෝදනා කළ සමහර කාන්තාවන් පසුව යම් ආශ්චර්යයකින් ගැබ් ගත් බව අපි උපකල්පනය කරමු. පූජ්‍ය පක්‍ෂයේ සාමාජිකයන් (සමහර අවස්ථාවල බෞද්ධ සහ කතෝලික) ද ප්‍රසිද්ධියේ නැතිනම් පුද්ගලිකව ජාතිවාදී මතයට පැන්නා.
බංකොලොත් හිඟන ජාතියක අපව අවුල් කිරීමට බොහෝ කලකට පෙර ශ්‍රී ලංකාව බවට පත් වූ කැත මෙයයි. මෙයින් අදහස් කරන්නේ කුමක්ද යන්න පිළිබඳ හොඳම අර්ථයෙන් මෙය හේතුව සහ ඵලය යැයි කෙනෙකුට තර්ක කළ හැකිය.

ෂාෆි ෂිහාබ්දීන් සිද්ධිය අප තුළම ඇති වරදේ රෝග ලක්ෂණයකි. එම අසමතුලිතතාවය නිවැරදි කිරීමට ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථා ප්‍රතිසංස්කරණය උපකාරී නොවේ. ජාතිවාදී කොඩිය ලෙළවමින්, රජරට විශ්වවිද්‍යාලයේ එතරම් ප්‍රසිද්ධ නැති ‘වියත්මඟ’ ශාස්ත්‍රපතිවරයා ජාතික දේශපාලන ප්‍රමුඛත්වයට පත් වූ අතර, පසුව ඔහුගේ අමාත්‍ය ධුරවල උපහාසයට ලක් විය.

එම අශුද්ධ කුමන්ත‍්‍රණයේ හවුල්කරුවන්, වගකිවයුතු රෝහල් නිලධාරීන් සහ පොලිස් නිලධාරීන් නීතියේ නොගැලපීම සිදු කළ අතර, වින්දිත වෛද්‍ය වෘත්තිකයා සහ ඔහුගේ පවුලේ අය ඔවුන්ට එරෙහිව එල්ල කරන ලද විෂ හමුවේ පැවැත්ම කළ නොහැකි බව සොයා ගත්හ. ඔවුන්ගේ සාමාජිකයින්ගේ අඛණ්ඩතාව ආරක්ෂා කිරීම සඳහා කතා කිරීමට බැඳී සිටින වෘත්තීය සංගම් විසින් ඉටු කරන ලද කාර්යභාරය කුමක්ද? ශ්‍රී ලංකා ප්‍රසව හා නාරිවේද වෛද්‍යවරුන්ගේ විද්‍යාලය (SLCOG) මේ සම්බන්ධයෙන් සැලකිය යුතු මැදිහත්වීමක් කළේද? නින්දිත ලෙස දේශපාලනීකරණය වී ඇති රජයේ වෛද්‍ය නිලධාරීන්ගේ සංගමය (GMOA) ගැන අඩුවෙන් කීවොත් වඩා හොඳය.
GMOA හි නායකත්වයේ මෑත කාලීන වෙනස එහි නායකත්වයේ වෘත්තීය අඛණ්ඩතාව ඇති කරනු ඇතැයි අපේක්ෂා කරමු. අද මේ ජාතිය මුළුමනින් ම ශෝකයෙන් හා වේදනාවෙන් කඩා වැටෙන විට, අපේ දුක්ඛිත තත්ත්වයට මේවා දායක හේතු වේ.
බල තණ්හාවෙන් සහ බල පිපාසයෙන් පමණක් කැපී පෙනෙන දේශපාලන පාලකයන් විසින් වගවීම අඟලක්වත් ලබා නොදෙන්නේ මන්ද?

ගාලුමුවදොර පිටියේ සාමකාමී විරෝධතාකරුවන්ට ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ප්‍රහාර එල්ල කළ මැති ඇමතිවරුන් කිහිප දෙනෙකු සහ ඔවුන්ගේ ආධාරකරුවන් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම සහ හිටපු අග්‍රාමාත්‍ය මහින්ද රාජපක්ෂ මහතා පිටව යාම යුක්තිය පසිඳලීම සඳහා එල්ල කරන ලද සෝබන පමණි.

මේ අතර, විසම්මුතිය මැඩලීමේ සමස්ත රාජ්‍ය මෙහෙයුමේ කොටසක් ලෙස විරෝධතාකරුවන් අත්අඩංගුවට ගැනීම, තර්ජනය කිරීම සහ බිය ගැන්වීම සිදු කෙරේ. මැයි 9දා ප්‍රචණ්ඩ ක්‍රියා අතරතුර එක් මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකුගේ මරණය, තවත් මන්ත්‍රීවරයෙකුට අනුකම්පා විරහිත පහරදීම සහ පොදු දේපළ විනාශ කිරීම, කෙසේ වෙතත්, සමහරක් රාජ්‍ය ඒජන්තවරුන් විසින්ම හෝ එහි ප්‍රතිඵලයක් ලෙස සිදු කිරීම පිළිබඳව තරුණ විරෝධතාකරුවන් විසින් නිසැකව හෙළා දැකිය යුතුව තිබුණි. අභ්‍යන්තර දේශපාලන පළිගැනීම් ගැන.

එසේ වුවද, එදින සිදු වූ සිදුවීම්වලින් මතු වූ විරෝධතාකරුවන්ගේ දෙපා යටින් සදාචාරමය උච්චතම ස්ථානය මාරු වීම, කුපිත වූ පිරිසට කුරුණෑගල පොලිසියේ නිලධාරීන් කළ අනතුරු ඇඟවීමෙන් මනාව පිළිබිඹු විය. ඉදිරියට යන රාජ්‍යයේ ක්‍රියා පටිපාටිය මෙයයි.
එමෙන්ම ‘අසාර්ථක’ නායකයෙකු ලෙස තමාට ඉවත්විය නොහැකි පදනම මත ජනාධිපතිවරයා නොසැලී ධූරයේ කටයුතු කරයි. මෙය සාමාන්‍ය බුද්ධියෙන් සහ ව්‍යවස්ථාපිත මූලධර්මයෙන් තොර අභූත සාධාරණීකරණයකි.

ව්‍යාපාරිකයකුට ඇඟිලි තුඩින් තඩිබාමින් සියලු ආකාරයේ මූල්‍යමය පිම්මකට, ජාතික ලැයිස්තුවෙන් පාර්ලිමේන්තුවට අශෝභන කඩිමුඩියේ කැටපල් කිරීම ජනාධිපති පාඨමාලාවේ පෙරළියක් නිසැකවම නොපෙනේ. ආණ්ඩුක්‍රම ව්‍යවස්ථාපිත ප්‍රජාතන්ත්‍රවාදයේ අංශු මාත්‍රයක් හෝ පිළිපැදීමක් මෙයින් පෙන්නුම් කරන්නේ නැත.

ඊට පටහැනිව, දේශපාලන සංස්ථාපිතයේ අභිමතය පරිදි ජාතික ලැයිස්තු මඩුල්ල පිරවිය නොහැකි බවට ශ්‍රේෂ්ඨාධිකරණය විසින් පැහැදිලිව දක්වා ඇති පූර්වාදර්ශ හමුවේ එය පියාසර කරයි. මෙම සියලු පත්වීම් සර්කස් ඇක්‍රොබැට් විසින් අසහනයට පත් ප්‍රේක්ෂකයින් සතුටු කිරීම සඳහා කරන මැජික් උපක්‍රමවලට සමාන ය.
ජනාධිපතිවරයා තමන්ව ‘මුදවා ගැනීමට’ බලාපොරොත්තු වන්නේ එලෙසද? ඇත්ත වශයෙන්ම, අනාගතයේ කුමක් සිදුවේද යන්න පිළිබඳ දැඩි අපේක්ෂාවෙන් අපි වෙව්ලන්නෙමු.

ta.title: “இந்த நாடு ஓரிரு தலைமுறைகளுக்குப் பின்னரும் எப்போதாவது சாம்பலில் இருந்து எழ வேண்டுமானால், தமிழ் மற்றும் முஸ்லிம் சிறுபான்மையினருக்கு எதிரான வெறுப்புணர்ச்சியால் ஊட்டப்படும் பெரும்பான்மை உணர்வுகள் அங்கீகரிக்கப்பட்டு நிராகரிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். அங்குதான் தேசிய சிகிச்சை தொடங்க வேண்டும்.
body_lines: முல்லைத்தீவில் இருந்து குருநாகல், அதுரிகிரிய வரை, எரிபொருளுக்காக பல நாட்களாகக் காத்திருக்கும் இலங்கையர்களின் பாம்பு வரிசைகளில் சீற்றம் வெடிக்கிறது. இறுதியாக, மக்களின் நம்பிக்கையை விட்டுக்கொடுத்த அரசாங்கத்திற்கு எதிராக சிங்களவர்கள், தமிழர்கள் மற்றும் முஸ்லிம்கள் ஆகியோரின் கூட்டு ஆழ்ந்த கோபத்தில் ஒற்றுமை பிணைக்கப்பட்டுள்ளது. ஆனால், பொதுச் சேவையில் பணிபுரியும் அவர்களின் கீழ்ப்படிதலுள்ள கையாட்களுடன் சேர்ந்து திருடர்கள் மற்றும் திறமையற்ற அரசியல் தலைவர்களின் கூட்டத்தால் டாலர் மற்றும் ரூபாய் கையிருப்பு பூஜ்ஜியமாகக் குறைக்கப்பட்ட நாடு ஆளுமை இல்லாத அபாயகரமான நீரில் மூழ்கும் போது இது மிகவும் தாமதமாகிவிட்டதா?

நிலத்தின் நீளம் மற்றும் அகலம் முழுவதும், பொதுமக்கள் எதிர்ப்புக்களுக்கு எதிராக காவல்துறை கட்டுப்பாடற்ற சக்தியுடன் செயல்படுவதால், பதட்டங்கள் அதிகரித்துள்ளன. பொதுமக்களின் கோபத்தின் மற்றொரு கொடிய அலையை அரசாங்கம் அழைக்கிறதா? இவ்வாரம் மாங்குளம் வெள்ளாங்குளம் வீதியில் பொலிஸாரைப் பார்த்துக்கொண்டிருக்கும் நிலையில் பெற்றோலுக்காக மக்கள் வரிசையில் காத்திருந்த காட்சிகளை படம்பிடித்துக் கொண்டிருந்த போது (தமிழ்) ஊடகவியலாளர் ஒருவர் இனந்தெரியாத ஒருவரால் தாக்கப்பட்டார்.

குருநாகலில், குறிப்பிட்ட எரிபொருள் நிரப்பு நிலையம் ‘சலுகையாளர்களுக்கு’ மட்டும் இரகசியமாக எரிபொருளை விநியோகிப்பதாக பாதிக்கப்பட்டவர் கூச்சலிட்டபோதும், ஒரு (சிங்கள) மோட்டார் சைக்கிள் ஓட்டுநர் பொலிஸ்காரரால் தட்டிக்கழிக்கப்பட முற்பட்டார்.

சுவாரஸ்யமாக ஒரு மூத்த போலீஸ்காரர், 'வன்முறையை ஏற்படுத்துவதற்கு மே 9ஆம் தேதி நடந்த சம்பவங்களை உதாரணமாக எடுத்துக் கொள்ளாதீர்கள்' என்று திரளான மக்களைக் கண்டித்துள்ளார். இலங்கையை ஆட்டிப்படைக்கும் சட்டம், பொருளாதாரம் மற்றும் நிர்வாகத்தின் கடுமையான நெருக்கடியைக் கட்டுப்படுத்துங்கள்.

உண்மையில், ஒரு தவறான பொலிஸ்காரர் தனது சட்ட அதிகார வரம்புகளை மீறுவதால், ஒரு தவறான குடிமகன் மட்டும் அல்ல. மாறாக, ஜனாதிபதி கோட்டாபய ராஜபக்ஷ, பிரதமர் ரணில் என நாட்டையே மூச்சுத் திணறடிக்கும் சக்தி வாய்ந்த சின்னம் இது. விக்கிரமசிங்க மற்றும் அமைச்சர்களின் அடியாட்கள் கீழ்நோக்கி இலங்கை பிரஜைகளின் துயரத்திற்காக பழியை சாமர்த்தியமாக திசை திருப்பும் கலையை கடைப்பிடிக்கின்றனர்.
அவரது பங்கில், பிரதமர் விக்கிரமசிங்க, கடந்த இரண்டு வருடங்களில் எடுக்கப்பட்ட பாழடைந்த கொள்கை முடிவுகளால் தற்போதைய நிதிச் சரிவுக்குக் காரணம் என்று கிளி சொல்வதன் மூலம் பொறுப்பில் இருந்து தப்ப முடியாது.

2019 மற்றும் 2020 ஆம் ஆண்டுகளில் இலங்கையின் ‘ராஜா-அரசியல்வாதிகளாக’ ராஜபக்சேக்கள் மீண்டும் ஆட்சிக்கு வந்ததற்கு விக்கிரமசிங்க-சிறிசேனா கூட்டணித் தலைமையின் கீழ் (2015-2019) பேரழிவுகரமான ஆட்சி தோல்விகள்தான் காரணம் என்பதை அவர் சுருக்கமாக நினைவுபடுத்த வேண்டும். 2019 ஆம் ஆண்டு தேவாலயங்கள் மற்றும் ஹோட்டல்கள் மீதான ஜிஹாத் தாக்குதல்கள், அரச முகவர்களின் நேரடி உடந்தையாக இல்லாவிட்டாலும், ராஜபக்சேக்கள் தங்கள் தேர்தல் பெரும்பான்மையை மகிழ்ச்சியுடன் குவிக்க வழிவகுத்தது.

இலங்கையின் தற்போதைய அவல நிலைக்கு ஒவ்வொருவரும் அவரவர் நியாயமான பங்களிப்பை வழங்கியிருந்தாலும், எந்தவொரு முன்னாள் தலைவருக்கும் கூறப்படுவதை விட அந்தப் பொறுப்பு மிகவும் அப்பட்டமானது. எப்படியிருந்தாலும், இலங்கையர்கள் (அப்பாவிகள் மற்றும் குற்றவாளிகள்) இப்போது தந்திரமான அரசியல் தலைவர்களால் ஒருமுறை, இருமுறை, மூன்று முறை அல்ல, பல முறை ஏமாற்றப்பட்டதற்காக தங்கள் ஏமாளிகளுக்கு மிகவும் பணம் செலுத்துகிறார்கள்.
முன்னாள் நிதியமைச்சர் பசில் ராஜபக்ஷ தனது அமைச்சுப் பதவியில் இருந்து வெளியேறும் போது, ​​தற்போதைய அரசியல்வாதிகளை பதவிக்கு தேர்ந்தெடுத்த மக்களே காரணம் என்று கூறியபோது, ​​இதனை ஒப்புக்கொண்டார்.

மெதமுலன அரசியல் குடும்பத்தை வழிபடும் தென்னிலங்கை மக்களுக்கு மகிந்த ராஜபக்ச ‘யுத்தத்தை முடிவுக்குக் கொண்டு வந்ததால்’ அதன் பாட்டன் குடும்பங்களை வழிபடும் மக்களுக்கு, குறிப்பாக செலவு அதிகம். ஆழமான தெற்கில் குடும்பங்கள் பட்டினியால் வாடுகையில், பசியுடன் தோற்றமளிக்கும் குழந்தைகள் சாலையோரங்களில் தங்கள் தாய்மார்களுடன் நின்று பிழைப்பதற்காக முருங்கைக்காய்களை விற்கிறார்கள். இந்த நெருக்கடியைத் தோற்றுவித்தவர்கள் தங்களைத் தவிர வேறு யாரும் இல்லை என்பதை சுய-உணர்தலின் சுவடு கூடத் தோன்றியிருக்கிறதா? இப்போதும் கூட எனக்கு உறுதியாக தெரியவில்லை.

ஆனால், ஓரிரு தலைமுறைகளுக்குப் பின்னரும் இந்த நாடு சாம்பலில் இருந்து எழவேண்டுமானால், தமிழ் மற்றும் முஸ்லிம் சிறுபான்மையினருக்கு எதிரான வெறுப்புணர்ச்சியால் ஊட்டப்படும் பெரும்பான்மை இனவாத உணர்வுகள் அங்கீகரிக்கப்பட்டு நிராகரிக்கப்பட வேண்டும். அங்குதான் தேசிய சிகிச்சை தொடங்க வேண்டும்.

சில நாட்களுக்கு முன்னர், டாக்டர் ஷாபி ஷிஹாப்தீன், சட்டவிரோத கருத்தடை அறுவை சிகிச்சை செய்ததாக பொய்யான குற்றச்சாட்டை முன்வைத்த குருநாகல் போதனா வைத்தியசாலைக்கு தனது இலங்கைக்கான சம்பள பாக்கியான ரூபா 2.67 மில்லியனை நன்கொடையாக வழங்கியதாக இலங்கை ஊடகங்கள் செய்தி வெளியிட்டுள்ளன. அத்தியாவசிய மருந்துகள் வாங்குவதற்காக இந்த நன்கொடை வழங்கப்பட்டது.

அந்த நேரத்தில், அவர் இனவெறி அரசியல்வாதிகள், மருத்துவமனை ஊழியர்கள் மற்றும் போலி போலீஸ்காரர்களால் சூனியம் செய்யப்பட்டார் மற்றும் கட்டாய விடுப்பில் அனுப்பப்பட்டார். இந்தக் குற்றச்சாட்டுகள் குற்றப் புலனாய்வுத் திணைக்களத்தால் (CID) முற்றிலும் ஆதாரமற்றவை என நிறுவப்பட்டது.

காயம் சேர்க்க, அவர் சட்டவிரோதமாக கருத்தடை செய்ததாக குற்றம் சாட்டிய சில பெண்கள், பின்னர் ஏதோ ஒரு அதிசயத்தின் மூலம் கர்ப்பமானார்கள், நாங்கள் கருதுகிறோம். மதகுருமார்களின் உறுப்பினர்களும் (சில சந்தர்ப்பங்களில் பௌத்த மற்றும் கத்தோலிக்கர்கள்) இனவெறிக் களத்தில் குதித்தனர், தனிப்பட்ட முறையில் இல்லாவிட்டாலும் பகிரங்கமாக.

வங்குரோத்து, பிச்சைக்கார தேசம் என்ற இந்த குழப்பத்தில் நாம் தள்ளப்படுவதற்கு நீண்ட காலத்திற்கு முன்பே, இலங்கை மாறிய அசிங்கம் இதுதான். இதன் பொருள் என்ன என்பதன் சிறந்த அர்த்தத்தில் இது காரணம் மற்றும் விளைவு என்று ஒருவர் உண்மையில் வாதிடலாம்.

ஷாபி ஷிஹாப்தீன் சம்பவம் நமக்குள் என்ன தவறு இருக்கிறது என்பதற்கான அறிகுறியாகும். அந்த ஏற்றத்தாழ்வை சரிசெய்ய அரசியலமைப்புச் சட்டங்களைச் சீர்திருத்தம் உதவாது. இனவாதக் கொடியை அசைத்து, ரஜரட்ட பல்கலைக்கழகத்தின் அதிகம் அறியப்படாத 'வியத்மக' கல்வியாளர் தேசிய அரசியல் முக்கியத்துவத்திற்கு உயர்ந்தார், பின்னர் அவரது அமைச்சர் பதவிகளில் கேலிக்குரிய பொருளாக மாறினார்.

அந்த புனிதமற்ற சதியில் பங்காளிகள், பொறுப்புள்ள மருத்துவமனை பணியாளர்கள் மற்றும் போலீஸ்காரர்கள் சட்டத்தின் பிடியில் சிக்காமல் சென்றனர். பாதிக்கப்பட்ட மருத்துவ நிபுணரும் அவரது குடும்பத்தினரும் தங்களுக்கு எதிராக வீசப்பட்ட விஷத்தின் முகத்தில் சுத்த இருப்பு சாத்தியமற்றதைக் கண்டனர். தங்கள் உறுப்பினர்களின் ஒருமைப்பாட்டைப் பாதுகாக்க பேசுவதற்குக் கட்டுப்பட்ட தொழில்முறை சங்கங்களால் என்ன பங்கு வகிக்கப்பட்டது? இலங்கை மகப்பேறியல் மற்றும் மகப்பேறு மருத்துவர்கள் கல்லூரி (SLCOG) இது சம்பந்தமாக ஏதாவது குறிப்பிடத்தக்க தலையீடு செய்ததா? இழிவான முறையில் அரசியல் மயமாக்கப்பட்ட அரசாங்க மருத்துவ அதிகாரிகள் சங்கம் (GMOA) பற்றி குறைவாகக் கூறுவது சிறந்தது.

GMOA இன் தலைமையில் சமீபத்திய மாற்றம் அதன் தலைமைத்துவத்தில் தொழில்முறை ஒருமைப்பாட்டைக் கொண்டுவரும் என்று நம்புகிறோம். இந்த தேசம் இன்று தனக்குள்ளேயே முழுக்க முழுக்க துக்கத்திலும் வேதனையிலும் சரிந்து கொண்டிருக்கும் நிலையில், இவையெல்லாம் நமது அவல நிலைக்குக் காரணமானவை.
அரசியல் ஆட்சியாளர்களால் தங்கள் மகத்தான பேராசை மற்றும் அதிகார தாகத்தால் மட்டும் ஏன் ஒரு அங்குலம் பொறுப்புக்கூறலை ஒப்புக்கொள்ளவில்லை?

காலி முகத்திடலில் அமைதியான முறையில் ஆர்ப்பாட்டத்தில் ஈடுபட்டவர்கள் மீது வன்முறைத் தாக்குதல்களைத் தூண்டிய சில அமைச்சர்கள் மற்றும் அவர்களது ஆதரவாளர்களின் கைதுகள் மற்றும் முன்னாள் பிரதமர் மஹிந்த ராஜபக்ஷவின் வெளியேற்றம் ஆகியவை நீதிக்கான அலறல்களை சாந்தப்படுத்துவதற்காக வீசப்பட்ட சோப்புகளாகும்.

இதற்கிடையில், எதிர்ப்பைக் கட்டுப்படுத்துவதற்கான ஒட்டுமொத்த மாநில நடவடிக்கையின் ஒரு பகுதியாக எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் கைது செய்யப்பட்டு, அச்சுறுத்தப்படுகிறார்கள் மற்றும் மிரட்டப்படுகிறார்கள். மே 9 வன்முறையின் போது ஒரு நாடாளுமன்ற உறுப்பினரின் மரணம், மற்றொருவரின் இரக்கமற்ற தாக்குதல் மற்றும் பொதுச் சொத்துக்கள் அழித்தல் போன்றவற்றுக்கு இளைஞர் எதிர்ப்பாளர்கள் சந்தேகத்திற்கு இடமின்றி கண்டனங்களை வெளியிட்டிருக்க வேண்டும். உள் அரசியல் பழிவாங்கல்கள்.

இருந்தபோதிலும், அன்றைய நிகழ்வுகளில் இருந்து வெளிப்பட்ட போராட்டக்காரர்களின் காலடியில் இருந்து தார்மீக உயரம் மாறுவது குருநாகல் காவல்துறையினரால் கோபமடைந்த கூட்டத்திற்கு விடுக்கப்பட்ட எச்சரிக்கையில் நன்கு பிரதிபலித்தது. இதுவே மாநிலத்தின் முன்னோக்கி செல்லும் செயல் முறை.

மேலும் ‘தோல்வியடைந்த’ தலைவராக இருந்து வெளியேற முடியாது என்ற அடிப்படையில் ஜனாதிபதி பதவியில் தொடர்ந்தும் தயங்காமல் இருக்கிறார். இது ஒரு அபத்தமான நியாயப்படுத்தல், பொது அறிவு மற்றும் அரசியலமைப்பு கோட்பாடு இரண்டும் அற்றது.

ஒரு தொழிலதிபரை தனது கொழுத்த விரல்களால் சகலவிதமான நிதிச் செலவுகளிலும், தேசியப்பட்டியல் மூலம் பாராளுமன்றத்திற்கு அநாகரீகமாக அவசர அவசரமாக அனுப்பியதில் ஜனாதிபதியின் பாடத்திட்டம் தலைகீழாக மாறியமை நிச்சயமாக நிரூபணமாகாது. இது அரசியலமைப்பு ஜனநாயகத்தை சிறிதளவும் கடைப்பிடிப்பதைக் காட்டவில்லை.

மாறாக, அரசியல் ஸ்தாபனத்தின் விருப்பத்திற்கும் விருப்பத்திற்கும் தேசியப் பட்டியல் இடங்களை நிரப்ப முடியாது என்பதற்கு உச்சநீதிமன்றம் தெளிவாக வகுத்துள்ள முன்னுதாரணங்களை எதிர்கொண்டு அது பறக்கிறது. இந்த சந்திப்புகள் அனைத்தும், ஈர்க்கப்படாத பார்வையாளர்களை மகிழ்விப்பதற்காக சர்க்கஸ் அக்ரோபாட்கள் செய்யும் மாயாஜால வித்தைகளுக்கு ஒப்பானவை.
இப்படித்தான் ஜனாதிபதி தன்னை ‘மீட்க’ நம்புகிறாரா? உண்மையாகவே, எதிர்காலம் என்னவாகும் என்ற பயங்கரமான எதிர்பார்ப்பில் நாம் நடுங்குகிறோம்.

en.title: “If this country is ever to rise from the ashes even a generation or two later, majoritarian sentiment fed by hate-mongering against Tamil and Muslim minorities must be recognised and rejected for what it is. That is where national healing must start.”
body_lines: From Mullaitivu to Kurunegala to Athurigiriya, fury erupts among snaking lines of Sri Lankans hopelessly waiting for days on end for fuel. Finally, unity binds the Sinhalese, the Tamils and the Muslims in their collectively deep anger against a Government that has forsaken public trust. But is this too late as the country heads into dangerously uncharted waters of non-governance with dollar and rupee reserves reduced to zero due to a cabal of thieving and incompetent political leaders along with their obedient henchmen in public service?

Across the length and breadth of the land, there is a discernible uptick in tensions as the police react with uncontrolled force against public protests. Is the Government inviting another deadly wave of public anger? This week, a (Tamil) journalist was attacked by an unidentified person while filming scenes of people waiting in lines for petrol on the Mankulam-Vellankulam road with the police looking on.

In Kurunegala, a (Sinhala) motorcyclist was literally sought to be throttled by a policeman even as the victim shouted that the particular fuel station concerned is distributing fuel in secret only to the ‘privileged.’

Interestingly a senior policeman reprimanded people flocking to the fracas to the effect that, ‘do not take the incidents of May 9th as an example to cause violence.’ This statement exposes the way that the State is authorising its agents to ‘manage’ and ‘control’ the acute crisis of law, economy and governance that is throttling Sri Lanka.

In effect, it is not just one stray citizen who is sought to be throttled by one stray policeman overstepping the bounds of his legal authority.Rather, this is a powerful symbol of the suffocation of the country itself as President Gotabhaya Rajapaksa, Prime Minister Ranil Wickremesinghe and Ministerial minions downwards practice the art of deftly deflecting blame to Sri Lankan citizens for their misery.
On his part, Prime Minister Wickremesinghe cannot escape responsibility merely by parroting that the present financial collapse was due to ruinous policy decisions during the past two years.

He must be summarily reminded that the return of the Rajapaksas to power as Sri Lanka’s ‘King-Politicians’ with grossly bloated majorities in 2019 and 2020 was due to disastrous governance failures under the Wickremesinghe-Sirisena coalition leadership (2015-2019). The 2019 jihadist attacks on churches and hotels with the inaction if not direct complicity of state agents led to the Rajapaksas amassing their electoral majorities with gleeful force.

That responsibility is far more stark than what can be attributed to any former leader, though each have contributed his or her fair share towards Sri Lanka’s present plight. Regardless, Sri Lankans (innocent and guilty alike) are now paying dearly for their gullibility in being fooled not once, twice, thrice but many times by crafty political leaders.

This was conceded as much by former Finance Minister Basil Rajapaksa on his way out from his ministerial portfolio when he said that the people who elected the current crop of politicians to power are to blame.

For the people of the South who worshipped the Medamulana political family led by its paterfamilias, Mahinda Rajapaksa because he ‘ended the war,’ the cost is particularly high. As families starve in the deep South, hungry looking children stand on roadsides with their mothers selling straggly bunches of drumsticks to survive. Has even a trace of self-realisation dawned that the originators of this crisis are none other than themselves? I am not quite sure, even now.

But if this country is ever to rise from the ashes even a generation or two later, majoritarian sentiment fed by hate-mongering against Tamil and Muslim minorities must be recognised and rejected for what it is. That is where national healing must start.

A few days ago, Sri Lankan media reported that Dr. Shafi Shihabdeen had donated his salary arrears of Sri Lanka Rs 2.67 million back to the Kurunegala Teaching Hospital which had falsely accused him of performing illegal sterilization operations. This donation was to buy essential medicines.

At the time, he was witchhunted by a coterie of racist politicians, hospital personnel and conniving policemen and sent on compulsory leave. These allegations were established to be completely unfounded by the Criminal Investigations Department (CID).

To add insult to injury, some women who had accused him of illegally sterilizing them, later became pregnant through some miracle, we assume. Members of the clergy (Buddhist and Catholic in some instances) also jumped on the racist bandwagon, privately if not publicly.

This was the ugliness that Sri Lanka had become, long before we were precipitated into this mess of a bankrupt, beggarly nation. One may indeed argue that this is cause and effect in the best sense of what that means.

The Shafi Shihabdeen incident is symptomatic of what is wrong in ourselves. Reforming Constitutions will not help to correct that imbalance. Waving the racist flag, a little known ‘Viyathmaga’ academic of the Rajarata University rose to national political prominence, later becoming an object of ridicule in his ministerial duties.

Partners in that unholy conspiracy, responsible hospital personnel and policemen went their way unscathed by the law while the victimised medical professional and his family found sheer existence impossible in the face of the venom directed against them. What role was played by professional associations bound to speak up to safeguard the integrity of their members? Did the Sri Lanka College Of Obstetricians & Gynecologists (SLCOG)make any significant intervention in this regard? Less said about the disgracefully politicised Government Medical Officers Association  (GMOA), the better.

Hopefully the recent change at the helm of the GMOA will bring about professional integrity in its leadership. As this nation collapses in utter grief and pain within itself today, these are contributory reasons for our plight.
Why has not an inch of accountability been conceded by political rulers distinguished only by their monumental greed and thirst for power?

The arrests of a few Ministers and their supporters who instigated violent attacks on peaceful protestors at Galle Face Green and the departure of former Prime Minister Mahinda Rajapaksa are but sops thrown to placate howls for justice.

Meanwhile, protestors are arrested, threatened and intimidated as part of an overall State operation to clamp down on dissent. On their part, youth protestors should also have issued unequivocal condemnation of the death of one parliamentarian, the merciless assault of another and the destruction of public property during the violence of May 9th, some of which was however by state agents themselves or as a result of internal political vendettas.

Nonetheless, the shifting of the moral high ground from under the feet of protestors emanating from the events of that day was well reflected in the warning issued by the Kurunegala policemen to the angry crowd. This is the modus operandi of the State, going forward.
And the President continues undeterred in office on the basis that he cannot leave as a ‘failed’ leader. This is an absurd justification, devoid of both commonsense and constitutional principle.

Presidential course reversal is certainly not evidenced in the catapulting of a businessman with his fat fingers in all kinds of financial pies, through the National List to Parliament in an indecent hurry as it were. This does not demonstrate the slightest adherence to constitutional democracy.

On the contrary, it flies in the face of precedents clearly laid down by the Supreme Court to the effect that National List slots cannot be filled at the whim and fancy of the political establishment. All of these appointments are akin to magic tricks performed by circus acrobats to please an unimpressed audience.
Is this how the President hopes to ‘redeem’ himself?  Truly, we shudder in dire expectation of what the future holds.
